title: Compiler-Driven Approximation Tuning for Hyperdimensional Computing
abstract: arXiv:2606.26547v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: As Moore's law reaches its physical and economic limits, domain-specific approaches are increasingly employed to accelerate machine learning workloads. Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) represents one such emerging paradigm, offering an alternative to conventional deep learning techniques. Rooted in cognitive models of computation, HDC is designed bottom-up with hardware efficiency as a first-class objective. HDC workloads map naturally to heterogeneous hardware platforms, including CPUs, GPUs, and FPGAs, as well as emerging in-memory computing technologies such as Resistive RAM (ReRAM) and Phase-Change Memory (PCM). HDC algorithms are intrinsically tolerant to noise and approximation, enabling substantial performance gains with minimal accuracy loss. In this work, we introduce ApproxHDC, a framework for automated identification and application of domain-specific approximations in HDC workloads. ApproxHDC extends the HPVM-HDC compiler i

translated_title: 面向超维计算的编译器驱动近似调优
translated_abstract: 随着摩尔定律达到其物理和经济极限，特定领域的方法越来越多地被用于加速机器学习工作负载。超维计算（HDC）代表了这样一种新兴范式，为传统深度学习技术提供了替代方案。HDC植根于计算的认知模型，从底层设计时就以硬件效率为首要目标。HDC工作负载自然地映射到异构硬件平台，包括CPU、GPU和FPGA，以及新兴的存内计算技术，如阻变存储器（ReRAM）和相变存储器（PCM）。HDC算法本质上对噪声和近似具有容错性，从而能够在精度损失极小的情况下实现显著的性能提升。在这项工作中，我们引入了ApproxHDC，这是一个用于在HDC工作负载中自动识别和应用特定领域近似的框架。ApproxHDC扩展了HPVM-HDC编译器。
tldr: 本文提出了ApproxHDC框架，通过编译器驱动的方式自动识别并应用超维计算工作负载中的特定领域近似，在保持极小精度损失的同时实现显著性能提升。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes ApproxHDC, a compiler-driven framework that automatically identifies and applies domain-specific approximations in Hyperdimensional Computing workloads, achieving significant performance gains with minimal accuracy loss.
